notable_pick_trades: Traded 6th-rounders for Keion White (from NE) and Brian Robinson Jr. (from WAS); traded 7th to HOU for DT Khalil Davis; conditional 5th to PHI for Bryce Huff. Expected 3 compensatory picks (two 4ths, one 5th) for losses of Aaron Banks, Charvarius Ward, and Talanoa Hufanga.
roster_context: Went 12-5 but suffered a 41-6 Divisional Round loss to Seattle, exposing depth deficiencies. Offensive core is aging and depleted — Aiyuk is being released, Jennings likely walking in FA, Kittle recovering from Achilles tear. Defense returns Bosa and Warner from injury but had a league-worst 20 sacks in 2025. New DC Raheem Morris (replacing Robert Saleh) shifts scheme. ~$49M in cap space but significant roster holes to fill. Still a contender with Purdy locked in long-term, but must inject youth and speed offensively while rebuilding the defensive front.

needs.explanation: The WR room is being gutted. Brandon Aiyuk is expected to be released on March 11 after a disastrous end to his tenure. Jauan Jennings (55 catches, 643 yards, 9 TDs in 2025) is a UFA projected to command $15-22M APY and is likely to depart. Kendrick Bourne and Skyy Moore are also UFAs. Ricky Pearsall is the only WR under contract but played just 9-10 games due to injuries in each of his first two seasons. Kyle Shanahan stated his top priority is adding speed on offense. The 49ers need at least two WRs, ideally a dynamic outside threat to replace Aiyuk at X and a complementary piece.
needs.depth: Extremely thin. Pearsall is the only notable WR under contract. Junior Bergen (2025 7th-rounder) is a return specialist with limited offensive upside. Demarcus Robinson was signed as a depth piece.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2, possibly double-dip with a Day 3 pick

needs.explanation: The 49ers finished dead last in the NFL with just 20 sacks in 2025. The interior defensive line desperately needs a pass-rushing presence. Jordan Elliott and Kevin Givens are both UFAs, leaving the interior reliant on rookies Alfred Collins (run-stuffer, not a pass rusher) and CJ West. The 49ers have not had a disruptive interior pass rusher since Javon Hargrave was released. Multiple mock drafts project SF taking a DT in Round 1. ESPN reports they want a 'disruptive interior rusher' and are targeting John Franklin-Myers in free agency.
needs.current_starter: Alfred Collins (2025 2nd-round pick), CJ West (2025 Day 3 pick)
needs.depth: Very thin after losing Elliott and Givens to FA. Khalil Davis is depth only. Collins is promising as a run defender but not a pass-rush threat.

needs.explanation: Trent Williams (age 37, $38.8M cap hit) and the 49ers have made no progress on a new contract. A release or post-June 1 cut is possible, which would leave the 49ers with no viable LT — their contingency is Austen Pleasants, a career practice squad player with one career start. SF hasn't drafted a tackle since 2021 (5th-round Jaylon Moore, who left in FA last year). Even if Williams returns via restructure, he turns 38 in July and the team must plan for a successor. Multiple mock drafts have the 49ers taking an OT in Round 1, including Monroe Freeling projections. This is existential for Brock Purdy's blindside protection.
needs.current_starter: Trent Williams (LT, contract standoff) / Colton McKivitz (RT, age 30)
needs.depth: Dire at LT. Austen Pleasants (one career start) is the only backup. McKivitz is adequate at RT but aging. No developmental tackles on roster.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if Williams is released; Round 2-3 even if he stays to develop successor

needs.explanation: Left guard was a revolving door in 2025 due to injuries. Both Spencer Burford and Ben Bartch are UFAs. Aaron Banks already left for Green Bay last offseason. Dominick Puni is solid at RG but was inconsistent at times. Center Jake Brendel is 33 and showing age. The 49ers need at least one starting-caliber interior OL, preferably a LG, and competition at center. Shanahan's outside-zone scheme demands athletic, mobile linemen.
needs.current_starter: LG: Vacant (Burford/Bartch both UFA), C: Jake Brendel (33), RG: Dominick Puni
needs.depth: Drake Nugent (UDFA) is a developmental interior prospect. No proven depth behind starters.

needs.explanation: Nick Bosa (torn ACL, Week 3) is tracking for training camp return but carries re-injury risk. 2025 first-rounder Mykel Williams also tore his ACL and is rehabbing. Bryce Huff has no guaranteed money left and could be a cap casualty. Yetur Gross-Matos (voided contract) and Clelin Ferrell are UFAs. Keion White is signed only through 2026. While Bosa and Williams returning healthy would solve the top-end talent issue, depth is paper-thin and the 49ers can't afford another situation where losing Bosa decimates the entire pass rush. Adding a complementary rusher is important insurance.
needs.current_starter: Nick Bosa (returning from ACL), Mykel Williams (returning from ACL)
needs.depth: Keion White (under contract through 2026), Bryce Huff (potential cap casualty). Gross-Matos and Ferrell are UFAs. Very thin behind the top two if healthy.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4, or FA signing (Trey Hendrickson linked)

needs.explanation: Cornerback is in flux. Renardo Green, the 2024 second-round pick, has an 80% chance of being traded per ESPN, as he may not fit new DC Raheem Morris's scheme. Charvarius Ward left last offseason. Darrell Luter Jr. is the other starter but the secondary was inconsistent late in 2025. The 49ers signed Siran Neal and Tre Brown but these are depth pieces, not starters. If Green is traded, CB becomes a tier-1 need immediately. Even if he stays, adding competition or an upgrade is warranted given his injury history (neck, hamstring, toe in 2025).
needs.current_starter: Renardo Green (trade candidate), Darrell Luter Jr., Deommodore Lenoir (nickel)
needs.depth: Siran Neal (signed, 2-year deal), Tre Brown (signed), Chase Lucas (RFA). Thin if Green is moved.

needs.explanation: George Kittle (age 32, turning 33 in Oct) tore his Achilles on January 11 in the Wild Card win over Philadelphia. Surgery went well and he's targeting an early-season return, possibly Week 1, but this is aggressive for an Achilles tear. He was limited to 11 games in 2025 even before the Achilles. Backup Jake Tonges emerged as a reliable fill-in (5 TDs) but is an RFA who could draw interest. Luke Farrell was signed but is a blocking TE. The 49ers need a developmental TE to plan for life after Kittle and to provide insurance if his recovery takes longer than hoped.
needs.current_starter: George Kittle (Achilles rehab, targeting early-season return)
needs.depth: Jake Tonges (RFA, could leave), Luke Farrell (blocking TE). Thin behind Kittle.

needs.explanation: Talanoa Hufanga signed with the Broncos last offseason. The 49ers signed Richie Grant and Jason Pinnock as replacements. Ji'Aire Brown showed promise as a 2025 draft pick. The position is not as dire as others but could use a true ballhawk capable of generating turnovers — the 49ers lack a game-changing safety. Adding a versatile safety who can play center field or drop into the box would complement the defense under Morris.
needs.depth: Jason Pinnock (signed), Tayler Hawkins. Adequate but unspectacular depth.

free_agency_impact: Releasing Brandon Aiyuk (expected March 11) — eliminates WR1-caliber player from roster, WR becomes critical need
Jauan Jennings (UFA, likely departing at $15-22M APY) — further guts WR room if he leaves
Trent Williams contract standoff ($38.8M cap hit, no progress) — may be released or require restructure, LT could become biggest need
Lost S Talanoa Hufanga to Denver Broncos (FA) — safety depth thinned
Lost LB Dre Greenlaw to Denver Broncos (FA) — LB depth reduced but Dee Winters emerged
Lost OG Aaron Banks to Green Bay Packers (FA) — LG now a clear hole
Lost CB Charvarius Ward (previous offseason) — secondary still adjusting
Signed QB Mac Jones (2-year deal) — backup QB settled, not a draft need
Signed CB Siran Neal (2-year deal) — CB depth added but not a starter solution
Signed WR Demarcus Robinson — depth piece only, not a WR1/WR2 answer
Signed S Richie Grant, S Jason Pinnock — safety depth addressed in FA
Signed CB Tre Brown, LB Luke Gifford, TE Luke Farrell — depth signings across multiple positions
Re-signed FB Kyle Juszczyk (2-year deal) — fullback retained for Shanahan's scheme
Re-signed LS Jon Weeks — special teams continuity
LG Spencer Burford (UFA), LG Ben Bartch (UFA) — both starting LG candidates set to depart
DT Jordan Elliott (UFA), DT Kevin Givens (UFA) — interior DL depth losses
EDGE Yetur Gross-Matos (voided), EDGE Clelin Ferrell (UFA) — edge depth losses
Renardo Green trade rumors (80% chance per ESPN) — could open major CB need if dealt
scheme_fit_notes: Kyle Shanahan's offense is built around the wide-zone run game, play-action, and YAC-centric passing. WRs must be able to create after the catch and play in the slot (Shanahan values versatile receivers). OL must be athletic, mobile blockers who can pull and reach-block. The 49ers under new DC Raheem Morris are expected to shift from Robert Saleh's Cover-3 base to a more diverse scheme. Morris's Falcons ran a 3-4 base with varied fronts. Interior DL pass rushers have elevated value as the 49ers desperately need someone who can collapse the pocket from inside to complement Bosa. EDGE prospects who can rush from multiple alignments (inside/outside) are especially valued. CB prospects should be able to play in both press-man and zone coverages given Morris's scheme diversity.
sources: 49ers.com official transactions and free agent tracker
